reasoning: All four open with a taste/bread idea, so I judged each slot on how well its remaining four spread across distinct conceptual axes. D wins: it is the only set whose every entry breaks a genuinely different and non-obvious assumption, and it lands the two most original concepts in the field, representing a day by subtraction/erasure (Vanishing Scent Map) and by a non-human swarm reading itself (Ant Almanac), plus the private-physiological-day reframe, which makes it the most fertile and generative. A is a close second with the boldest single swing (the Indifferent Tide decentering humans to geological scale) and the strongest prose, but its smell and sound entries are more conventional gradient/ambience ideas than D's reframes. C ranks third on the strength of two bold reframes (the blanket that has mass and weighs on you, and a stranger's live heartbeat as your day), though its scent-front and heartbeat partly echo ideas elsewhere. B is last: the most concrete and cleanly written set, but conceptually the safest, leaning on temperature gradients and decay without the assumption-breaking leaps that distinguish D and A.
